title: What gets calculated first
checkLabels.t5: The income is not typed in
why.untouched: script.js is exactly as it was.
hint2.untouched: The income comes out right. The other two numbers are not what they should be.
why.not-closed: A tag in index.html is open and never closed.
why.not-linked: The script never reaches the page.
why.crashed: Your code stopped with an error.
hint2.crashed: Look at the Console panel below.
why.typed-answer: You typed the answer in instead of calculating it.
hint2.typed-answer: Tomorrow the prices will be different. The sum has to work with any numbers.
hint2.wrong-income: That line was correct to begin with. If you changed it, put it back.
hint2.wrong-profit: The rent is 200 lev, all of it. Read the line slowly and see what happens to it before it gets subtracted.
hint3.wrong-profit: Division happens before subtraction. 420 - 200 / 2 is not (420 - 200) / 2. The first gives 320, the second gives 110.
hint2.wrong-average: The average is the income divided by all the cups sold. There are 100 cups.
hint3.wrong-average: Division happens before addition, so the income is divided by one number only, and the other gets stuck on top. Brackets say what happens first.
hint4.wrong-average: Put brackets around the two quantities being added.
why.wrong-count: There are not three lines in the console.
why.lost-p: A line has disappeared from the page.
blocks.0.text: Three sums. Income, profit, average price per cup.
blocks.1.text: The income comes out right: 420. The other two are wrong, and nothing breaks. The profit shows 320 on a rent of 200, and the average price is 70 lev a cup.
blocks.3.text: Multiplication and division happen before addition and subtraction. Not left to right — the strong ones first, then the weak ones.
blocks.5.text: The first gives 14, the second gives 20. The same numbers, the same signs, a different order.
blocks.7.text: Whatever is inside brackets is calculated before everything else. That is the only way to impose your own order.
blocks.8.items.1: then multiplication and division, left to right
blocks.8.items.2: then addition and subtraction, left to right
blocks.9.text: The rule is the same as at school. The difference is that there the mistake is visible, and here the number simply comes out different.
blocks.10.text: Write brackets even when you do not need them
blocks.11.text: Nobody remembers the whole order by heart, and the list is far longer than the three lines above. Brackets do not slow the code down and cost nothing.
blocks.12.text: Code you read without hesitating is better than code that is correct and makes you think.
blocks.14.text: Fix the two wrong sums. The profit is the income minus the whole rent. The average is the income divided by all the cups sold.
blocks.15.text: The numbers 420 and 220 should not appear in the code. They are results, not data.
